summary_one_line: Reach Indic matra-reordering with a pre-base matra under a non-Malayalam/non-Tamil script; broad havoc byte exploration finds it where I2S keeps anchoring Malayalam/Tamil.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An input whose shaped buffer has script != HB_SCRIPT_MALAYALAM AND != HB_SCRIPT_TAMIL while still entering the pre-base-matra block (start+1<end && start<base), i.e. an Indic-classified syllable that is neither Malayalam nor Tamil. The false branch at L1149 requires the script to be any OTHER Indic script.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive resolves 10/10 with avg hitcount 74 on the branch. Its broad havoc/byte mutation explores a wide range of script-tag byte combinations: winning seeds carry diverse non-Malayalam/non-Tamil byte content (e.g. 'rafc', 'radupe', 'an-' ASCII runs and varied 0x0c/0xcb/0xbd fills) that drive the buffer into the false (non-Malayalam, non-Tamil) branch (L1149 W: T=0 F=29).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog blocks 9/10 with avg hitcount 0; at L1149 the loser only ever takes the TRUE side (L1149 L: T=14 F=0), meaning cmplog inputs keep producing Malayalam or Tamil scripts. cmplog's I2S substitution copies CMP operands (e.g. script-tag/codepoint constants) back into the input, repeatedly anchoring the buffer onto the Malayalam/Tamil branch rather than diversifying away from it.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: The winner is naive, whose plain coverage-guided havoc/token mutation stack (havoc-only, no comparison introspection) performs broad undirected byte exploration of the script/codepoint bytes, hitting the common non-Malayalam/non-Tamil false branch. cmplog's added input-to-state operand substitution is counter-productive here: it pins CMP operand values that bias the buffer toward the Malayalam/Tamil true side, so the divergence is attributable to the loser's comparison-solving technique making it worse, not to any comparison the havoc-only winner had to solve.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked false side is the non-Malayalam/non-Tamil script branch at L1149.
The globally blocked side is the false branch.
naive is the winner and cmplog is the loser of the only decisive pair, with delta I2S.
naive resolves the branch in all trials while cmplog is blocked in nine.
The block requires entering the pre-base-matra region first.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: if (start + 1 < end && start < base) /* Otherwise there can't be any pre-base matra characters. */

evidence_trail.claim: The winner takes the false (non-Malayalam/non-Tamil) side while the loser only takes the true side at L1149.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L1149  T=0 F=29  T=14 F=0  if (buffer->props.script != HB_SCRIPT_MALAYALAM && buffer...

evidence_trail.claim: The winner hits the branch 74 times on average while the loser never hits the resolving side.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=74  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: Winning naive seeds carry diverse ASCII/byte content driving varied scripts.
cmplog's mutator stack adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes one CMP operand for another at matching input offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive uses an undirected havoc + token mutation stack with no I2S.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: claimed_mechanism is havoc-only, not I2SRandReplace, so no lineage query is mandated. The winner here is naive (the loser is cmplog), so the resolving technique is naive's plain havoc/token byte exploration; the I2S delta belongs to the losing arm and is the suspected cause of cmplog being WORSE (anchoring onto the Malayalam/Tamil true side), not a comparison the winner had to solve.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where the script tag is selected by an exact integer compare against HB_SCRIPT_MALAYALAM/TAMIL and cmplog resolves the non-equal (false) branch at least as fast as naive across trials, which would show I2S helps rather than hinders reaching this branch.

weakest_evidence_point: The mechanism that pins cmplog onto the Malayalam/Tamil true side is inferred from I2S operand-substitution behavior and the L1149 T=14 F=0 loser pattern; no seed lineage directly shows an I2S substitution forcing a Malayalam/Tamil script tag.
